titleStmt.title: A Transcription of John in Codex Rehdigeranus
VL11
The New Testament
The Gospel according to John
john
titleStmt.funder: The Arts and Humanities Research Council

sponsor.name: The Institute for Textual Scholarship and Electronic Editing

publisher.name: The Institute for Textual Scholarship and Electronic Editing
University of Birmingham

availability.p: Licensed under a Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International License (CC BY 4.0): available for re-use provided attribution is made to the original creators (the IGNTP and any named participants) and an indication is given of any changes made. See further .

msItem.title: Quattuor Euangelia
Four Gospels in order Mt Lc Jo Mc

listBibl.bibl: Black and white photographs in ITSEE, supplied by Vetus Latina-Institut.
H.J. Vogels (ed.), Codex Rehdigeranus (Collectanea Biblica Latina 2), Rome 1913

projectDesc.p: This transcription was made by the Verbum Project as part of an online and printed edition of the Old Latin versions of John between 2003 and 2010.

editorialDecl.p: This transcription covers ff. 242r to 296r. Lacunose in 1:1-1:16, 6:32-61, 11:56-12:10, 13:34-14:22, 15:3-15, 16:13-21:25. Ammonian sections and chapter numbers are present, with arcades indicating parallel passages at the foot of the page. There are many lengthy corrections to this manuscript, often in the margin.The transcription includes foliation, lineation, all punctuation and abbreviations. Ammonian sections and chapter numbers are included. It is not always possible to make out rubrication from our black and white images. The initial transcription file was a plain text file for use with COLLATE. Further information about the procedures followed may be found in the electronic edition at
This second edition of the file was converted to XML by Catherine Smith using the project's Python conversion scripts 1.0.
The XML specifications used are version 1.5 of the IGNTP Guidelines available at

revisionDesc.change: Adjustment of transcription to version 1.5 of the XML by Hugh Houghton.
Reconversion of the original plain text file using Python scripts to version 1.4 of the XML by Catherine Smith and manual checking.
Hyphen entity replaced by lb attribute break="no" by Hugh Houghton
Changes made in order to display properly with VLI.xsl transformation by Hugh Houghton
Transcription converted to the TEI XML schema and corrected for parsing by Hugh Houghton

ab.note: dni in smaller letters
The scribe has miscopied the Ammonian section number, writing xcii for xuii (92 for 17).
the correction uncertain
first hand text from Vogels
The scribe has miscopied the Ammonian section number, writing xciiii for xuiiii (94 for 19).
Hole in parchment extending over two lines.
Hole in parchment extending over two lines.
Note the change of page numbers. Folio 249r contains Luke 22:69 to 23:4; Folio 257v is blank
Note that this rubricated text is corrected to 'interrogabant'
Vogels marks the 't' of 'et' as written in rasura.
Vogels marks the 'tes' of 'quaerentes' as written in rasura.
Two folios, containing John 6:32-61, are missing.
Vogels marks the 'c' of 'cum' as written in rasura.
Vogels marks the 'r' of 'erat' as written in rasura.
Vogels marks the 'l' of 'bethlee' as written in rasura, as if the copyist initially put bethe...
'fine¬:' is added by a later hand in the central margin after 'dicens'.
Vogels marks 'cati' of 'iudicatis' as written in rasura.
Vogels marks the 'a' of 'patre' as written in rasura.
Vogels marks 'tae' of 'prophetae' as written in rasura.
This Ammonian section is written in a later hand and darker ink than the others.
There is a cross after templo and before the next line.
Vogels reads 'interrogate¬' but no nasal bar is visible on our images.
Vogels marks the 'dis' of 'uidisti' as written in rasura.
Vogels marks the 't' of 'dicunt' as written in rasuram.
There is a cross after the word 'ih¬s' which appears to relate to a few indecipherable words in the left-hand margin in a later hand; the last of these is 'cum'.
Vogels reconstructs this line as: 'multa signa facit'.
Vogels reconstructs this line as: 'si dimittimus eu¬'
Vogels reconstructs this line as: 'sic omnes cre'
Vogels reconstructs the whole line as: 'dent in eum et'
Vogels reconstructs this line as: 'gens pereat'
Vogels reconstructs this line as: 'Hoc autem a semet'
Vogels reconstructs this line as: 'ipso non dixit sed'
Vogels reconstructs this line as: 'cum esset pon'
The original writing has been erased; a second hand appears to have written ih¬s in the gap, but it is not clear from our images.
One folio is missing, which would have contained John 11:57-12:9
Vogels reconstructs this line as: 'rex israhel'
Vogels reconstructs this line as: 'Et inuenit ih¬s'
Vogels reconstructs this line as: 'asellum et sedit'
Vogels reconstructs this line as: 'super eum'
Vogels reconstructs this line as: 'peribebat turba'
Vogels reconstructs this line as: 'quae erat cum eo'
Vogels reconstructs this line as: 'quando lazaru¬'
Vogels reconstructs this line as: 'uocauit de mo'
There is a cross in the left hand margin with a lectionary indication.
Vogels reconstructs this line as: 'sequatur me'
Vogels reconstructs this line as: 'Et ubi ego ero'
Vogels suggests that these deleted letters could have read 'et' or 'est'
Vogels reconstructs this line as: 'et iterum'
Vogels reconstructs this line as: 'clarificabo'
Vogels reconstructs this line as: 'Turba ergo quae'
Vogels reconstructs this line as: 'credebant in eu¬'
Vogels reconstructs this line as: 'Ut sermo esaiae'
Vogels reconstructs this line as: 'et sanem illos'
Vogels reconstructs this line as: 'Haec dixit esaias'
Vogels reconstructs this line as: 'quando uidit ma'
Vogels reconstructs this line as: 'uita aeterna est'
Vogels reconstructs this line as: 'quae ergo loquor'
Vogels reconstructs this line as: 'iudas simonis'
Vogels reconstructs this half-line as: 'scarioth'
Vogels reconstructs this half-line as: 'sciens'
Vogels reconstructs this line as: 'quia omnia'
Vogels reconstructs this line as: 'dit ei pater'
Vogels reconstructs this line as: 'manus. et quia'
Vogels reconstructs this line as: 'recubuisset'
Vogels reconstructs this line as: 'ita et uos'
Vogels reconstructs this line as: 'faciatis'
Vogels reconstructs this line as: 'ille supra pectus'
Vogels reconstructs this line as: 'ih¬u dicit ei'
Vogels reconstructs this line as: 'quid dixerit ei'
Vogels reconstructs this line as: 'quidam enim'
Two folia are missing, covering John 13:34-14:23
sic; the word is split 'quem admodum' in the manuscript
Because of the erasure in the manuscript, the word division is 'omnes arme¬tum'
One folio is missing, containing John 15:3-15
There is a small cross in the margin at the end of this line, and a large asterisk at the beginning of the next line.
Vogels does not record these erased letters.
The manuscript ends with folio 296v
